title: pittosporum glabratum
intro: Pittosporum glabratum, also known as the Miro tree or Manuka-kiekie, is a small evergreen tree native to the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of the Pittosporum glabratum have been used in herbal medicine for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Pittosporum glabratum include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate respiratory problems. Pittosporum glabratum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
